plang.note: Run 1 green (0 FAIL). Run 2 hit a single FAIL: Modules/Http/StreamCallback — '502 Bad Gateway' from an external endpoint (24.5s). Flaky external infra; coder v4 touched no HTTP code. NOT a coder-v4 regression.

coverage.note: Changed production files (number/this.Arithmetic.cs power cap, types/Loader.cs sealed gate, math/sqrt.cs) all have line coverage; the gap is branch/path coverage on Loader's sealed gate — see F1.

findings.issue: The sealed-name security gate has THREE enforcement sites (pass-1 explicit [PlangType] @ Loader.cs:88, ITypeRenderer-registration pass @ :122, inferred-name branch @ :101). Only the pass-1 explicit-attribute site is tested (LoadDll_AttemptToShadowSealedName via IdentityShadow.dll). SealedNames_AreCaseInsensitive is purely structural (asserts set contents). No test exercises the renderer-registration gate or the inferred-name gate.
findings.impact: Renderer-substitution is the EXACT attack the SealedNames docstring names ('replaced identity's CLR type OR ITS RENDERER could produce authentically-signed envelopes whose body was attacker-composed'). A future refactor that drops the :122 or :101 check would let a runtime-loaded DLL register a renderer for `identity` (or shadow it via the @this-convention inferred name) and silently change how the signing-load-bearing type serializes — with zero test going red. MUTATION-CONFIRMED: neutralizing BOTH the :122 and :101 checks leaves all 9 RuntimeTypeLoadingTests green (0 failed).
findings.suggestion: Add two tests: (a) a fixture ITypeRenderer with TypeName="identity" -> Loader.Register must return Success=false, ErrorKey="TypeLoadCollision"; (b) a `this`-named class in a namespace whose leaf is a sealed name (inferred-name path), or a direct fixture exercising the InferName branch -> assert TypeLoadCollision. Mirror the existing IdentityShadow assertions (Success/ErrorKey/ErrorMessage).

findings.issue: Sqrt_NegativeInput_Fails asserts only result.Success.IsFalse(). The codeanalyzer-F2 fix changed the negative-sqrt error key from 'InvalidInput' (old handler pre-check) to 'ArithmeticError' (now from number.Sqrt via Wrap). The handler test does not assert the key, so it cannot tell the difference — it would pass under any failure.
findings.impact: The handler-boundary contract (negative sqrt surfaces 'ArithmeticError', the single canonical key the F2 fix promised) is unpinned at the handler. MUTATION-CONFIRMED: forcing DoSqrt to throw DivideByZeroException ('DivideByZero' key) keeps Sqrt_NegativeInput_Fails GREEN. Not a false green for the fix overall — NumberUnaryTests.Sqrt_NegativeNumber_SurfacesArithmeticError pins Error.Key at the number layer and DID go red under the same mutation. So coverage exists; the handler test is just weak.
findings.suggestion: Add `await Assert.That(result.Error?.Key).IsEqualTo("ArithmeticError");` to Sqrt_NegativeInput_Fails so the handler-boundary key is pinned, not just the number-layer one.

findings.issue: coder v3 and v4 shipped no baseline-tests.md (only v2 has one). Character workflow requires the coder to record pre-edit test state each version; tester must flag a missing baseline as a process violation.
findings.impact: Regression vs pre-existing-failure attribution had to be reconstructed from v2's baseline (8a2cd23cb: 3609 C# / 246 plang green) plus this session's two plang runs. Low impact this turn because the v2 baseline was all-green, but the discipline should hold.
findings.suggestion: coder writes baseline-tests.md at the start of every version, not just v2.

findings.issue: Post-F2, the class docstring (lines 13-15) still says negative sqrt 'surfaces a typed validation error inside the handler, not this method.' That is now inverted: the handler pre-check was deleted and DoSqrt itself throws ArithmeticException. Not a test defect — a stale comment that will mislead the next reader about where the validation lives.
findings.impact: Documentation drift on the exact behavior F2 changed; could lead a future contributor to re-add a handler pre-check 'because the doc says so', resurrecting the double-guard codeanalyzer F2 removed.
findings.suggestion: Update the docstring to: 'negative input throws ArithmeticException here, surfaced as ArithmeticError via Wrap; the handler no longer pre-checks.'

summary: Coder v4 (security F1 power CPU-DoS cap + F2 Loader sealed-name allowlist; codeanalyzer-v2 F1 cap-scoping + F2 sqrt single-key + F3 doc) plus the mathhelper-deletion unary retype. C# suite 3634/3634 green; the power-cap tests are genuinely strong (boundary, just-over-cap typed failure, Math.Pow-skip pins for double/fractional). Two real test-quality gaps confirmed by mutation: (F1, major) the Loader sealed-name security gate is enforced at 3 sites but tested at only 1 — neutralizing the ITypeRenderer-registration and inferred-name checks leaves all RuntimeTypeLoadingTests green, and renderer-substitution is the documented threat; (F2, minor) the sqrt handler test asserts only Success.IsFalse so the F2 error-key change is unpinned at the handler boundary (it IS pinned at the number layer). Minor process/doc items (F3 missing baseline, F4 stale docstring). The single plang FAIL was an external 502 Bad Gateway on an HTTP streaming test — flaky infra, green on the prior run, unrelated to coder v4. Verdict needs-fixes on F1+F2.
